fund.name: ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund (erstwhile Bluechip Fund) - Direct Plan - Growth
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Stable core holding in large-cap stocks of well-established companies. ICICI is consistent with low fees. Suitable as a steady anchor for moderate risk investors.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large and Midcap Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Offers growth potential by investing in both large and mid-sized companies. Strong track record over 3-5 years. Helps diversify beyond only large caps with higher return expectation.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Small Cap Fund - Direct - Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Adds exposure to fast-growing smaller companies for long-term upside. Higher risk but justified due to 22-year horizon. Motilal Oswal has shown skill in stock selection here.

fund.name: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: Flexible allocation across market caps allows fund manager to adapt to changing market conditions. HDFC Flexi Cap is consistent, widely trusted, and tax-efficient.

fund.name: Aditya Birla Sun Life Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund-Direct Growth
fund.fund_house: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Limited

allocations.rationale: Low-cost way to capture mid-cap market performance. Index funds reduce human error risk. Great for long-term goals when paired with active picks.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Gives global diversification by tracking top US companies. Helps reduce country-specific risk. Historically strong performer over long periods.

fund.name: Sundaram Diversified Equity Fund - Direct Plan - Growth

allocations.rationale: One-time investment using existing surplus to immediately earn returns. Well-diversified fund with proven track record across market caps.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww, Kuvera, or Coin. Step 2: Start the monthly SIPs as listed for each fund. Step 3: Transfer ₹1.25L lumpsum to start the diversified equity fund. Step 4: Set calendar reminders for SIP start confirmation and review dates.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm all SIPs are active and amounts are correct.
After 1 year: Check if any fund has underperformed its peers over 3 years.
Year 3 and every 3 years: Review child education costs and adjust SIPs if needed.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check if any category has grown more than 5% above or below your target. For example, if small cap grows from 10% to 16%, sell some and move into underweight categories to restore balance.
plan.rationale: At 36 with a 22-year horizon, you can afford a mix of high-growth and stable funds. We use flexi-cap and multi-cap funds for flexibility, add US exposure via S&P 500 for global diversification, and emphasize tax-efficient equity schemes given your 30% tax bracket. This plan balances strong growth with smart risk control to target ₹8Cr by retirement.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will temporarily fall. This is normal and expected over long periods.
Small-cap and mid-cap funds are more volatile — they can fall harder in downturns but also rise faster in good times.
US funds depend on dollar strength and US market performance — both can move independently of Indian markets.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

aps.reasoning: Passive-instrument-fraction: only 20 % of equity is in index funds (Midcap 150 + S&P 500 index funds); the remaining 80 % is in active categories (large-cap, large-&-mid, flexi-cap, multi-cap, small-cap) → 0.15. Turnover: annual rebalancing threshold of ±5 % and 3-year review cycles → largely buy-and-hold → 0.75. Cost-emphasis: expense ratios of active funds 1.0–1.2 % are accepted without pushback; low-cost angle is mentioned only for the two index funds → modest cost focus → 0.3. Research-vs-cost: rationales cite manager skill, track record, and stock-selection ability ("Motilal Oswal has shown skill", "fund manager can adapt") rather than broad-market exposure → research orientation → 0.2. Time-horizon: 22-year goal,耐受 volatility, rare reviews → long-term aligned → 0.85. Portfolio-activeness: categories are active mandates with undisclosed risk metrics; even the large-cap allocation is an active fund; only 20 % is passive → portfolio judged active → 0.25.

pqs.reasoning: Good 22-year horizon match and diversified equity mix, but plan is 100 % equity with no debt/liquid sleeve to buffer the 1 Cr emergency goal or moderate risk appetite; projected 12.6 % return is optimistic for an all-equity blend and no step-up SIP is suggested despite rising education costs. International and index exposures add diversification; all allocations are internally consistent and rebalancing is clear. Tax section rightly calls equity funds favourable but ignores the 30 % slab investor’s need for an 80C fill (no ELSS) and offers no debt or arbitrage/hybrid option whose gains would still be equity-taxed, so tax efficiency is only partial.
